Departmental intent
Horizontal intent
→ We identified two kinds of AI demand

Vertical vs. horizontal intent

VERTICAL INTENT
[Department | Industry]
People who are searching for a department, industry, or specific use case.
Examples
AI for marketing, HR agents, AI hiring, social media agent
Competitors
Harvey Clay Jasper
HORIZONTAL INTENT
[AI capability led]
People who are searching for a specific capability or task, persona agnostic.
Examples
Project management, agent builder, notetaker, vibe, AI research, AI reporting, AI writing
Competitors
Relevance Notion Base44
Proof: Gartner — vertical AI revenue will significantly outpace horizontal AI
Click to view source →
Proof: ~2.5× more investment in Vertical AI than Horizontal AI ($4,609M vs $1,821M)
Click to view source →

Marketing
Total monthly search: 33,810
Persona
One department, shared mission — drive revenue through awareness, demand, and pipeline — but many functions and ways of working: CMO, Brand & Creative, Content & Comms, Growth/Demand Gen, Product Marketing, Marketing Ops, Email & Lifecycle, Field & Events, Analytics, Regional, Agency/Vendor Management.
Main promise
Drive more demand with the AI work platform that executes marketing work from brief to launch.
Value drivers in the AI era
  • Ship on-brand campaigns at the speed the business needs
  • See what's working, and where to invest next
Pain points
  • Team can't execute fast enough — and AI is making it worse
  • Leaders can't see where budget, time, and impact are going
Example agents
Social Media
End-to-end post creation and scheduling.
Campaigns
Streamlining strategic planning and campaign management.
Content Creation
Copywriting, video reels, and visual creatives.

Copy.ai
